review_summary: Textual grounding is sufficient for concept-focused questions, and no separate video clip is needed.

question_bank.question: A model almost fits on one GPU, and batch size 1 fits when using mixed precision. What is the recommended next step before escalating to full parameter sharding?
choices: Use ZeRO stage 2, because it is treated as a low-cost way to reduce gradient and optimizer-state memory.
Immediately switch to ZeRO stage 3, because stage 2 is only for inference.
Disable mixed precision, because full precision always reduces memory.
Use CPU-only training, because multi-GPU methods cannot help once batch size 1 fits.
question_bank.explanation: The practical recipe starts with mixed precision. If a batch of one fits, ZeRO stage 2 is the next efficient memory-saving choice before using the more communication-heavy stage 3.

question_bank.question: What defines parameter-efficient fine-tuning?
choices: Updating only a small subset of parameters instead of all model parameters.
Training a full model from scratch using fewer examples.
Using only CPU memory while keeping every parameter trainable.
Replacing the pretrained model with a smaller hand-written rule system.
question_bank.explanation: PEFT keeps most pretrained weights fixed and adapts the model through a small number of trainable parameters, reducing memory and storage requirements.

question_bank.question: Why can PEFT be attractive even when full fine-tuning technically fits in memory?
choices: On small datasets, constrained updates may reduce overfitting and generalize better than changing every parameter.
PEFT guarantees higher accuracy on every task by using more parameters than full fine-tuning.
PEFT removes the need for any labeled data during fine-tuning.
PEFT is only useful for models that were not pretrained.
question_bank.explanation: When the task dataset is small relative to the model, limiting the number of trainable parameters can act like regularization while still allowing task adaptation.

question_bank.question: Which broader concern is used to motivate efficient training besides avoiding out-of-memory errors?
choices: Efficiency affects who can participate in model development and reduces compute and environmental costs.
Efficiency is only relevant because GPUs cannot run neural networks in FP32.
Efficiency mainly matters for making generated text more creative.
Efficiency is needed because large models cannot be evaluated automatically.
question_bank.explanation: Efficient methods make experimentation more accessible and reduce wasted computation, which also matters for environmental impact and concentration of model-building power.
